content.full_text:
TO: [ ] JEANNE   [ ] JAY
[XX] LYNN   [ ] BARBARA
[ ] RICH   [ ] BOB S.
[XX] HELEN   [XX] OTHER Bob B.

FROM: J. Pritchard
DATE: 6/17/83

[XX] TELEPHONE ADVICE - SEE BELOW
[ ] CORRESPONDENCE ADVICE - SEE ATTACHED DRAFT
(IF YOU HAVE ANY COMMENTS, NOTIFY ADVISOR WITHIN 1 DAY)

FILE LOCATION: Sections 84211(g) and 84216

INCLUDE IN ADVICE PACKAGE: YES [ ] NO [XX]

CALLER Jim Gross
REPRESENTING Dobbs & Nielsen
PHONE NUMBER:

QUESTION: Is a major donor required to disclose on his or her campaign statement the fact that he or she has co-signed for a loan for a candidate?

ANSWER: No. A major donor has no obligation to report on his or her campaign statement that he or she has co-signed for a loan for a candidate. (In addition, co-signing for a loan does not count toward qualifying a person as a committee.) If a major donor becomes liable for repayment of a loan (i.e., the candidate defaults), then the major donor must report the outstanding balance as a loan (a contribution) to the candidate.

The recipient of the loan, however, must report the name, address, occupation and employer of the co-signer
